purpose: Two-certificate Gate A: controlled matched identifiability plus a separately executed online-policy-feasible diagnosis certificate.

online_policy_feasible_certificate.action_selection: select eight whole public-summary blocks per transition channel from predictive-fit samples only; normalize each diagonal log likelihood to one effective dimension over the actual selected feature dimension; require a non-saturated information spread; expand the selected set by calibrated single-recipe information; then execute the set in canonical frozen action-library order as one fixed phase-invariant cyclic schedule

paired_phase_design.budget_interpretation: Budget 2 is the online-history-aligned stress point with two protocol-available pre-change references. Gate A controls at budget 4; the controlled certificate uses four matched references and four post-change diagnostics, while the online certificate retains the actual two-reference history and receives four post-change experiments.

dimension_normalization_fit_confirmation.interpretation: One effective likelihood dimension is divided by the actual selected coordinate count: 32 for one evidence channel, 64 for an online two-recipe batch, and 128 for a controlled budget-four batch. The same rule removes EIG saturation in both formal tasks without task-specific temperatures.

online_batch_selection_confirmation.selection_interpretation: The selected batch may vary with predictive-fit nuisance samples, but both registered development fits produce fully ordered, non-saturated rankings whose spreads exceed the preregistered 0.05-nat floor.

preliminary_fixed_scale_online_policy_co.interpretation: This preliminary development check used likelihood scale 0.125 before the dimension-normalized rule was finalized. It is retained transparently and does not control RC20. At n=20, no-change recall 18/20 has Wilson lower bound 0.69897 and therefore narrowly misses the formal 0.70 family threshold.

dimension_normalized_online_policy_confi.interpretation: This is a development accuracy check under the final dimension-normalized rule, not a formal gate result. At n=20, material-law and rate-law recall 18/20 each have Wilson lower bound 0.69897 and therefore narrowly miss the formal 0.70 family threshold. RC20 remains fixed at n=30 on fresh namespaces.

development_calibration.selection_basis: RC19 showed that the controlled 608-coordinate diagonal likelihood saturated all budget-four batch EIG values at ln(4), making active selection arbitrary even though rate-law cells were perfectly identified. Whole-summary-block selection alone removed saturation for reaction-to-crystallization but not for the easier electrochemical families. Before RC20 held-out execution, the task-agnostic final rule was therefore fixed as one effective likelihood dimension divided by the actual selected feature dimension. On the same registered development fit namespace, all 15 controlled and online batch values became distinct for both tasks, with spreads above the preregistered 0.001-nat floor. No RC20 held-out outcomes were used for calibration.

public_feature_contract.source: agent-visible numeric observation and reward after every compiled public recipe operation in the controlled matched and actual hidden-change campaigns
public_feature_contract.encoding: Each complete public experiment is summarized task-agnostically into four-value blocks (ever finite, first finite, last finite, finite range) for every sorted public observation scalar and reward. Controlled and online certificates select eight whole blocks per evidence channel from predictive-fit samples only. Each diagonal log likelihood is normalized to one effective dimension over the actual selected feature count. Controlled batches use matched post-minus-pre summaries. Online first observations use absolute summaries, followed by a latent reference-time mixture of baseline-to-candidate transition contrasts and same-candidate stable contrasts. Controlling batch rankings must exceed their preregistered non-saturation spread.
public_feature_contract.active_action_source: prior expected information over frozen k-recipe batches after predictive-fit-only whole-block selection and inverse-selected-feature-dimension likelihood normalization; the online policy pre-ranks four recipes before held-out execution and cycles through them without phase input
public_feature_contract.fixed_trajectory_action_source: first k actions in the frozen deterministic action library

supersession_reason: RC19 preserved a passing online-policy certificate but failed the controlled matched certificate because reaction material-law and topology recall were each 25/30. Audit showed that the controlled oracle fitted 608 correlated coordinates from 24 samples per candidate/action and assigned all 15 budget-four batches the exact prior entropy ln(4), so its claimed active selection was arbitrary. RC20 retains the physical tasks, interventions, action library, budgets, thresholds, and no-change controls. It selects whole public-summary blocks from predictive-fit data only, normalizes diagonal evidence by the actual selected feature dimension, and fails closed when a controlling information ranking remains saturated. The dimension rule is shared by both tasks and both certificates. All RC19 and earlier evidence remains immutable.

rate_law_response_certificate.pass_rule: Every frozen public catalyst-dose response must remain within the declared multiplier envelope and the set must contain responses on both sides of unity.

decision_relevance_certificate.pass_rule: For every required metric, median max effect must exceed its threshold and either old-policy regret or optimal-action change rate must exceed its threshold.
